title: picea balsamea var. fraseri
intro: Picea balsamea var. fraseri, also known as Fraser fir, is a coniferous tree species native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its dark green, glossy needles and compact, symmetrical shape. The medicinal properties of Fraser fir include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Fraser fir include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and boost the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Fraser fir can be prepared as an herbal tea, essential oil, or infused in a salve, making it a versatile plant for various health applications.
